text.title: Three-Tier Tool Classification Enforced at Execution
text.summary: Every tool the agent can call carries one of three tier labels enforced by the executor, not the prompt.

section.title: Closed tier vocabulary, default to T2
section.p: The tool registry assigns each tool to T0 (read-only), T1 (safe mutation, audit-required), or T2 (destructive, human approval required). The executor reads the label at call time. Tools missing from the registry resolve to T2 — unknown tools are dangerous tools.

rule.statement: The tool executor MUST classify every callable into T0/T1/T2; MUST allow T0 unconditionally with audit; MUST allow T1 unconditionally with audit; MUST refuse T2 unless a signed approval token is supplied; MUST treat any tool absent from the registry as T2; and MUST NOT honor tier overrides from the agent prompt or tool arguments.
rule.rationale: Centralizing the gate at the executor makes safety a property of the system rather than the prompt. Prompt-level "ask before deleting" instructions are routinely bypassed by jailbreaks, confusion, or follow-up tool-use loops; a registry lookup is mechanical.

example.note: Single chokepoint; missing tool defaults to most-restrictive; audit is mandatory.
The agent controls its own gate. A confused or jailbroken LLM passes `approved=true` and the call goes through.

antipattern.description: Granting "auto" status to a T2 tool because "the team trusts the agent now".
antipattern.reason: Trust is not a property of the tool — it is a property of the action's blast radius. Failover and delete remain T2 forever; only the speed of approval improves.

section.reference: Maniak — human-in-the-loop tier patterns for kagent.
